data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 11 AM ET: Tariff Ruling Is a Setback for Trump but Doesnâ€™t End Trade War | U.S. ...
data.description:
Tariff Ruling Is a Setback for Trump but Doesn't End Trade War

Trade experts say the president is unlikely to abandon levies as the principal tool in his quest to rewrite the rules of global commerce.

----

U.S. GDP Shrank Less Than First Reported Last Quarter. This Is Why.

The Bureau of Economic Analysis released its second estimate for first-quarter GDP growth Thursday morning.

----

U.S. Jobless Claims Rose Last Week

In the week through May 24, 240,000 people newly filed for unemployment benefits, compared with 226,000 the week prior and economists' expectations of 230,000 claims.

----

Stocks Move Higher After Court Blocks Many of Trump's Tariffs

Global markets rallied and U.S. stocks rose, after a federal trade court struck down President Trump's sweeping tariffs.

----

South African Reserve Bank Cuts Main Repo Rate to 7.25%

The South African Reserve Bank cut its main repo rate by 25 basis points to 7.25% on Thursday, as tensions with the U.S. continue amid ongoing global uncertainty.

----

Bank of Korea Cuts Rates, Slashes 2025 Growth Outlook

South Korea's central bank cut its policy rate in a widely expected move to support the country's sagging economy.

----

University Endowments Are Breaking Up With Private Equity. It's Hard to Do.

It once seemed like the perfect investment-offering better returns than stocks with less risk. Lately, though, private equity has become an albatross.

----

One of the best performing stock markets this year is facing a big risk

The outcome of Sunday's presidential vote is more uncertain than investors had hoped.

----

Trump's Nuclear Orders Could Loosen Radiation Standards. DOGE's Key Role.

The president wants to speed up construction of reactors. But experts are worried about the safety measures that keep the public safe.

----

The â€˜TACO Trade' That Has Trump Fuming

The president says the "Trump Always Chickens Out" claim is wrong and tariff changes are a negotiation.

----

Trump's Tariffs Slam Eastern Europe's Detroit

The U.S. president's trade policies are exposing the fragility of an economic model built on car production and jeopardizing growth, jobs and standards of living.

----

The Fed Forecasts Stagflation

Officials at their May 6-7 meeting reaffirmed the need for a "cautious approach" given increased uncertainty over tariff policies.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Financial Services Headlines at 11 AM ET: South African Reserve Bank Cuts Main Repo Rate to 7.25% | Japanâ€™s ...
data.description:
South African Reserve Bank Cuts Main Repo Rate to 7.25%

The South African Reserve Bank cut its main repo rate by 25 basis points to 7.25% on Thursday, as tensions with the U.S. continue amid ongoing global uncertainty.

----

Japan's NTT Unit Plans to Buy Online Bank in $5.1 Billion Deal

The wholly owned mobile-carrier unit of NTT intends to launch a tender offer to purchase shares of SBI Sumishin Net Bank and take it private.

----

Vance Vows to Champion the Crypto â€˜Movement'

Speaking at a bitcoin conference in Las Vegas, the vice president said President Trump would sign a bill that establishes rules for stablecoins.

----

Trump Officials Keep Talking About Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac. Confusion Reigns.

Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac have been under government conservatorship since the 2008 financial crisis.

----

The Fed Forecasts Stagflation

Officials at their May 6-7 meeting reaffirmed the need for a "cautious approach" given increased uncertainty over tariff policies.

----

Seaside Equity Amasses More Than $720 million for New Funds

Seaside Equity Partners is wrapping up two new funds that will add more than $720 million in fresh capital to the San Diego-based firm's investment coffers, according to people familiar with the process.

----

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on UniCredit, Societe Generale, and more in the latest Market Talks covering financial services.

----

Bank of Montreal Raises Dividend After Rise in Quarterly Earnings

Bank of Montreal bumped up its dividend after earnings in its second-quarter were lifted by strength in its wealth management business.

----

UniCredit to Double Stake in Greece's Alpha Bank to Around 20%

The move deepens UniCredit's partnership with one of Greece's largest lenders at a time when its expansion efforts in Germany and Italy are facing roadblocks.

----

Telegram Set to Raise $1.5 Billion in Bond Issue Despite CEO's Legal Woes

The messaging app is set to raise funds from both new and existing backers.

----

This Vanguard Bond Fund Is Taking Some Calculated Risks

Corporate bonds, securitized debt, and foreign bonds are among the $13.8 billion Vanguard Core Bond fund's recent buys.

----

Garcia's Take: Asset Light or Asset Heavy? The Question Shaping Private-Markets Players

Publicly traded private-equity firms, usually seen as alike, diverge to pursue two very different growth strategies.

----

Spain to Further Review BBVA's Bid for Sabadell

The offer for the Catalan lender was to be referred to the cabinet on general-interest grounds other than competition, citing the potential impact on the sector, territorial cohesion and social policy.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Energy Headlines at 11 AM ET: Crude Futures Give Up Tariff-Ruling Gains | U.S. ...
data.description:
Crude Futures Give Up Tariff-Ruling Gains

Oil futures turned lower after spiking overnight on the court's ruling against Trump's tariffs and the market still appeared to be focused on OPEC+ meeting this weekend.

----

U.S. Natural Gas Storage Increases By 101 Bcf

U.S. natural gas futures remained lower following a storage report that showed an increase in inventories that surpassed estimates.

----

NRG's Stock Is a Top Gainer. How Does It Really Make Money?

The company looks more like a risky energy trader than a simple power producer.

----

Shell to Buy TotalEnergies' Stake in Nigeria Oil Field for $510 Million

The stake increases Shell's interest in the deep-water Bonga field to 67.5% from 55%.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Adani Power, National Grid and more in the latest Market Talks covering energy and utilities.

----

Controversial New York Pipeline Project Is About to Be Revived

Pipeline operator Williams will seek to build two natural-gas supply lines to New York following talks between President Trump and Gov. Kathy Hochul.

----

Trump's Nuclear Orders Could Loosen Radiation Standards. DOGE's Key Role.

The president wants to speed up construction of reactors. But experts are worried about the safety measures that keep the public safe.

----

Oil Giants Face Mix of Climate Pressures at Annual Investor Meetings

One year after Exxon Mobil sued activist shareholders pressing it on climate issues, the company touted that the energy transition is "an opportunity."

----

Why Big Oil Turned to Trump for Help in Africa

Chevron, ConocoPhillips and other companies are locked in a dispute with six Central African countries over fossil-fuel projects.

----

Chevron Gets Narrow License to Preserve Oil Assets in Venezuela

The company can maintain key infrastructure but is barred from importing oil from the South American country.

----

Enel Increases U.S. Renewable Energy Capacity in Swap, $50 Million Cash Deal

The energy company said the deal with Gulf Pacific Power would increase its consolidated renewable capacity in the U.S. by 285 megawatts.

----

Why Exxon and Chevron Are Fighting So Hard Over an Oil Project in Guyana

The feud has chilled the relationship between their CEOs. It comes to a head when arbitration starts Monday.

----

â€˜It's Time for Nuclear' to Meet Growing U.S. Power Needs, Trump Declares

The administration expects nuclear reactors to be tested and deployed within the president's term.

----

Trump Wants to Sink Offshore Wind. This Project Could Test His Reach.

Dominion Energy's massive project off the coast of Virginia would be a boon for U.S. jobs and energy needs-not to mention the climate. Its future hangs in the balance.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 11 AM ET: Kohlâ€™s Results Top Prior Forecast; 2025 Outlook Maintained | United, ...
data.description:
Kohl's Results Top Prior Forecast; 2025 Outlook Maintained

Kohl's posted first-quarter results slightly ahead of its previous expectations, a bright spot for a company facing a difficult operating environment and recently ousted leadership.

----

United, JetBlue Form Alliance to Sell Seats on Each Other's Flights

The arrangement will give United long-sought access to JFK, while JetBlue fliers will get access to a broader network of destinations.

----

Best Buy Stock Falls After Earnings. The Retailer Trimmed Guidance Because of Tariffs.

The retailer says both domestic and international comparable sales slip from the prior year.

----

Foot Locker Swings to Loss Ahead of Acquisition by Dick's

Foot Locker posted weaker results in its latest quarter, extending a challenging period for the company ahead of a planned buyout by Dick's Sporting Goods.

----

Senators Bash Nvidia's Plans for Facility in China

Sen. Jim Banks and Sen. Elizabeth Warren are accusing the chip maker of undercutting national security.

----

Japan's NTT Unit Plans to Buy Online Bank in $5.1 Billion Deal

The wholly owned mobile-carrier unit of NTT intends to launch a tender offer to purchase shares of SBI Sumishin Net Bank and take it private.

----

NRG's Stock Is a Top Gainer. How Does It Really Make Money?

The company looks more like a risky energy trader than a simple power producer.

----

Ethan Allen Faces Fewer Tariffs Than Rivals, but Consumer Worries Still Bite

Ethan Allen's decision decades ago to make most of its couches, chairs and more in North America should be paying off as the rapidly shifting global trade environment roils others in the industry. So far, it isn't.

----

Foxconn Reassures Shareholders Amid Cloudy Outlook

The Taiwan-based company plays an increasingly important role in building AI servers for U.S. tech giants such as Amazon and Nvidia.

----

Li Auto Posts Profit Rise, Slightly Higher Revenue

The Chinese automaker's net profit in the first quarter was better than expected but it guided for softer revenue growth ahead as it continues to face tough competition in China's overcrowded EV market.

----

Costco Earnings Are Here. What They'll Show.

Investors still believe the retailer is a haven from macroeconomic uncertainty.

----

Shell to Buy TotalEnergies' Stake in Nigeria Oil Field for $510 Million

The stake increases Shell's interest in the deep-water Bonga field to 67.5% from 55%.

----

Dell Reports Earnings Today. The AI Server Maker's Guidance Might Disappoint.

Dell Technologies will report its latest quarterly earnings after the market close Thursday.

data.title: 5 Top Rated Gold IRA Companies in 2025 (Best Gold IRA Companies List Released)
data.description:
GoldInvestmentPlanner.com has published its revised ranking of the best gold IRA companies for 2025.

DALLAS, TX / ACCESS Newswire / May 29, 2025 / GoldInvestment Planner has released its updated list of the top-rated gold IRA companies.

Nathan Schwartzman, Chief Editor at GoldInvestmentPlanner.com, notes, "Gold has performed exceptionally well in recent years, with optimistic projections suggesting it could reach $3,357 by year-end, and some analysts anticipating highs of $3,720.38. This presents an excellent opportunity to explore leading gold IRA companies to enhance your retirement portfolio."

Here's a brief overview of the top-rated companies:

  • Augusta Precious Metals (Top Choice Overall): Arrange a complimentary online session with a Harvard-trained economist.

  • Goldco (Best for Guided IRA Rollover): Receive a free guide to retirement savings.

  • American Hartford Gold (Most Competitive Pricing): Qualify for up to $15,000 in free silver with a gold IRA.

  • Birch Gold Group (Great for New Investors): Request a free gold IRA starter kit.

  • Noble Gold (Low Entry Investment): Access a free gold IRA information pack.

The data paints a compelling picture. Roughly 11% of Americans hold gold, and 18% view it as a strong long-term investment. Precious metals continue to draw savvy investors. During uncertain economic periods, platforms like Robinhood saw a spike in gold fund investments.

Top best gold IRA companies offer transparent details, reasonable fees, and outstanding customer service. With several excellent options available, you can diversify your retirement portfolio effectively.

Augusta Precious Metals

Formed in 2012, these days Augusta Precious Metals ranks as a top rated company in the gold IRA industry. The firm concentrates exclusively on gold and silver investments, choosing to deepen its expertise in precious metals rather than diversifying into other asset types.

Key Features of Augusta Precious Metals

Augusta distinguishes itself among top gold IRA companies through several notable offerings:

  • Education-First Philosophy: Augusta prioritizes investor knowledge before transactions. Led by Devlyn Steele, a Harvard-educated economist with 36 years of financial experience, the company provides extensive educational tools. These include webinars, articles, and guides to help investors navigate potential pitfalls in the gold IRA market.

  • Personalized Customer Care: Every client is paired with a dedicated customer success agent who streamlines the process by managing most paperwork and offering ongoing support.

  • Lifelong Account Assistance: Augusta fosters long-term relationships by providing continuous access to its education team, IRA processing staff, and order desk, even after the initial investment.

  • Industry Accolades: Recognized by Money Magazine as the "Best Overall Gold IRA Company," Augusta holds an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau and a AAA rating from the Business Consumer Alliance, with no recorded complaints-a rare achievement in the precious metals sector.

  • Open and Honest Practices: Unlike some competitors who obscure fees or risks, Augusta clearly outlines its costs, account terms, and potential investment challenges, empowering clients with full transparency.

Pricing and Fees at Augusta Precious Metals

Augusta's fee structure remains competitive, though its minimum investment requirement is higher than many peers:

Fee Type

Amount

One-time setup fee

$50

Annual maintenance fee

$80-$125

Annual storage fee

$100 (non-segregated) / $150 (segregated)

First-year total costs

$225-$275

Recurring annual costs

$200-$225

A minimum investment of $50,000 is required to open a gold IRA, which is higher than some competitors but allows fees to be spread across a larger asset base. For example, annual fees of $225 on a $50,000 investment equate to roughly 0.45% per year.

Additional costs to consider:

  • A 5% markup on precious metals purchases (approximately $2,500 on a $50,000 investment).

  • Wire transfer fees, typically $25-$50 per transaction.

  • A buyback fee of less than 1% when selling metals back.

Augusta covers shipping and insurance for qualifying purchases, a cost-saving benefit not always offered by competitors.

Pros and Cons of Augusta Precious Metals

Pros:

  • Exceptional educational materials and individualized support.

  • No complaints filed with BBB or BCA.

  • Transparent pricing with no hidden fees.

  • The team handles nearly all paperwork.

  • Free shipping and insurance on qualifying orders.

  • Dependable buyback program.

Cons:

  • High $50,000 minimum investment.

  • Pricing details require a phone call, not listed online.

  • Fees are slightly higher than some competitors.

  • Refunds are available only for first-time buyers.

Who Should Choose Augusta Precious Metals?

Augusta Precious Metals caters to investors with significant retirement funds. Its $50,000 minimum investment targets higher-net-worth individuals rather than those just starting their retirement journey.

The company is ideal for:

  • Knowledge-Seeking Investors: Those who appreciate Augusta's emphasis on education, backed by insights from a Harvard-trained economist and robust resources.

  • Long-Term Planners: Investors focused on sustained retirement growth benefit from Augusta's ongoing support and relationship-driven approach.

  • Those Who Value Transparency: Clients who prioritize clear, upfront information about fees, risks, and account terms.

  • Stability-Oriented Retirees: Augusta's focus on gold as a hedge against market fluctuations appeals to those safeguarding savings, as seen during the 2022 market volatility.

With its superior service, comprehensive educational tools, and commitment to transparency, Augusta Precious Metals earns its place as a top gold IRA company for those with the financial capacity to meet its requirements.

Goldco

Goldco secures its position as a top rated gold IRA company, consistently ranking highly among leading firms. It delivers outstanding service to individuals aiming to diversify their retirement portfolios with precious metals. Over the years, Goldco has assisted thousands of clients in transferring retirement funds into gold IRAs, earning a strong 4.8/5 rating from over 1,500 Trustpilot reviews, reflecting its solid standing in the precious metals market.

Key Features of Goldco

Goldco distinguishes itself through several compelling features:

  • No Minimum Investment: For 2025, Goldco has removed all minimum investment thresholds, making it the only major gold IRA company with no upfront cost requirement. Previously, a $25,000 minimum applied, but this change opens precious metals investing to a broader audience.

  • Education-Centric Approach: Goldco emphasizes investor education, offering a complimentary Wealth Protection Kit, along with articles and market updates to help clients understand gold IRAs and economic trends.

  • Diverse Product Range: The company offers a wide selection of IRS-approved precious metals for IRAs, including Gold American Eagle, Gold American Eagle Proof, Gold Liberty, Gold Phoenix, and Gold Independence Hall coins, available in both gold and silver.

  • Top Buyback Program: Goldco's standout feature is its buyback guarantee, promising to repurchase metals at the highest market rate, providing investors with enhanced liquidity and confidence.

  • Streamlined Rollover Process: Goldco simplifies transferring retirement accounts, such as 401(k)s, 403(b)s, TSPs, and traditional IRAs, into self-directed precious metals IRAs while preserving tax benefits. Their team manages most paperwork for a hassle-free experience.

Pricing and Fee Structure

Goldco employs a flat-fee model that becomes increasingly cost-effective as investments grow:

Fee Type

Amount

Account setup fee

$50

Annual storage fee (non-segregated)

$100

Annual storage fee (segregated)

$150

Goldco partners with trusted depositories like Brink's Global Services and Delaware Depository, with an optional Texas Depository for an additional $50 annually. All facilities provide advanced security and full insurance through Lloyd's of London.

For IRA deposits of $50,000 or more, clients can receive 5% to 10% back in free silver, a valuable incentive that sets Goldco apart in the competitive precious metals market.

Pros and Cons of Goldco

Pros:

  • No minimum investment requirement.

  • Industry-leading buyback guarantee at the highest market price.

  • Free silver bonus for qualifying deposits.

  • Flat fees regardless of account size.

  • A+ BBB rating and AAA Business Consumer Alliance rating.

  • 4.8/5 Trustpilot score from over 1,590 reviews.

  • Ranked 317 on Inc's 2024 Fastest-Growing Private Companies list with 1,349% growth.

  • Live chat and phone support available.

Cons:

  • Fee details require contacting a representative, not listed online.

  • Limited to gold and silver, excluding platinum or palladium.

  • Some clients report pressure to purchase additional products.

  • No free storage fee option.

  • No mobile app for account management.

Who Should Choose Goldco?

Goldco's tailored features make it an excellent choice for:

  • New Investors: With no minimum investment and a focus on education, Goldco provides an accessible entry point for beginners, supported by comprehensive resources and expert guidance.

  • Budget-Conscious Investors: The flat-fee model benefits those with larger portfolios, minimizing costs compared to percentage-based fee structures.

  • Retirement Account Transfers: Goldco excels at guiding clients through rollovers, ensuring IRS compliance and managing paperwork, ideal for those diversifying retirement assets.

  • Liquidity-Focused Investors: The buyback guarantee offers flexibility, allowing clients to sell metals at competitive rates when needed.

  • Wealth Preservation Seekers: Goldco appeals to those aiming to shield savings from market volatility and global economic shifts, prioritizing long-term stability over short-term profits.

Through its exceptional customer support, educational resources, and investor-friendly policies, Goldco solidifies its reputation as a leading gold IRA company, catering to clients of all portfolio sizes seeking to invest in precious metals.

Noble Gold Investments

Noble Gold Investments ranks sixth among the best gold investment companies in our evaluation. The company distinguishes itself through its commitment to accessibility and preparedness, catering to a wide range of investors. With over 20 years of combined expertise, Noble Gold's team has facilitated more than $1 billion in precious metals transactions, helping clients diversify their retirement portfolios effectively.

Key Features of Noble Gold Investments

Noble Gold offers several standout features that set it apart in the gold IRA market:

  • Royal Survival Packs: These unique, pre-curated precious metals bundles, ranging from $10,000 to $500,000, are designed for liquidity during economic crises, offering a practical solution for emergency preparedness.

  • Noble Express Home Delivery: Through this service, clients can receive their precious metals at their doorstep within five days of purchase, providing convenience and speed.

  • Premium Customer Service: Noble Gold delivers exceptional support, with knowledgeable staff offering personalized guidance to ensure a seamless investment experience.

  • Offshore Storage Option: For clients seeking storage outside the U.S., Noble Gold partners with International Depository Services in Mississauga, Canada, providing secure, international storage solutions.

Pricing and Storage Options

Noble Gold maintains competitive and transparent pricing:

Fee Type

Amount

Custodian maintenance

$125 annually

Segregated storage

$150 annually

Minimum IRA investment

$2,000

Storage fees remain consistent regardless of investment size, making Noble Gold cost-efficient for larger accounts. All metals are held in segregated storage, ensuring they are individually assigned to clients and never combined with other holdings.

Pros and Cons of Noble Gold Investments

Pros:

  • Low $2,000 minimum investment, ideal for beginners.

  • No-fee buyback program available to all clients.

  • Free silver coins offered for qualifying new accounts.

  • A+ BBB rating with strong customer feedback.

Cons:

  • Royal Survival Packs are limited to North America.

  • Full product pricing requires contacting a representative, not available online.

  • Storage and maintenance fees increase overall costs.

Who Should Choose Noble Gold Investments?

Noble Gold is well-suited for investors seeking flexibility and security in their precious metals investments. It's particularly ideal for:

  • Beginner Investors: The low $2,000 minimum makes it accessible for those new to gold IRAs or with limited funds.

  • Emergency-Prepared Investors: The Royal Survival Packs appeal to those concerned about economic instability, offering quick access to physical metals during crises.

  • Cost-Conscious Clients: Flat storage fees benefit investors with larger portfolios, reducing proportional costs.

  • Diversification Seekers: Investors looking to add precious metals to their retirement plans value Noble Gold's straightforward process and expert support.

With its focus on accessibility, emergency readiness, and top-tier customer service, Noble Gold Investments is a strong choice for those prioritizing flexibility and affordability in their gold IRA investments.

Conclusion

Here's a concise summary of the top rated gold IRA companies for 2025:

  • Augusta Precious Metals (Top Choice Overall): Arrange a complimentary online session with a Harvard-trained economist.

  • Goldco (Best for Guided IRA Rollover): Receive a free guide to retirement savings.

  • American Hartford Gold (Most Competitive Pricing): Qualify for up to $15,000 in free silver with a gold IRA.

  • Birch Gold Group (Great for New Investors): Request a free gold IRA starter kit.

  • Noble Gold (Low Entry Investment): Access a free gold IRA information pack.

In 2025, these top gold IRA companies offer distinct advantages for retirement investors. Augusta Precious Metals leads with its robust educational tools and stellar industry reputation, though it requires a $50,000 minimum investment.

Goldco follows closely, eliminating minimum investment barriers and offering a strong buyback program, making precious metals accessible to a wider audience.

American Hartford Gold stands out with transparent pricing and attractive promotions.

Birch Gold Group appeals to newcomers with a modest investment minimum and wide variety of precious metals, while Noble Gold Investments rounds out the list with a $2,000 entry point and unique survival packs designed for economic preparedness.

Your financial circumstances and investment objectives should shape your decision when selecting from these top-rated companies. Minimum investment thresholds range from $2,000 to $50,000, so available capital is a key factor.

Pay close attention to fee structures: flat fees are more cost-effective for larger portfolios, while percentage-based fees may better suit smaller accounts. Each company offers varying levels of educational support and customer service, from personalized agents to comprehensive self-guided resources.

With gold prices hitting a record $3,509.90 per Troy ounce in April 2025, precious metals continue to prove their value as a shield against inflation and market swings. While historical performance doesn't predict future outcomes, gold remains a vital component of a diversified retirement strategy. To make an informed choice, request information packets from several companies, compare their fees and offerings, and review customer feedback and industry ratings.

These five companies shine as the gold IRA market grows. Their commitment to transparency, fair pricing, and excellent customer support sets them apart. Whether you're a seasoned investor or just beginning your retirement journey, these companies offer a solid entry point into precious metals investing.

Frequently Asked Questions

What are some of the most top rated gold IRA companies in 2025?

Based on thorough evaluations, the top-rated gold IRA companies for 2025 are Augusta Precious Metals, Goldco, American Hartford Gold, Birch Gold Group, and Noble Gold Investments. Each company offers distinct advantages, catering to investors seeking to diversify their retirement portfolios with precious metals.

What factors should I evaluate when selecting a gold IRA company?

When choosing a gold IRA company, key considerations include minimum investment thresholds, fee structures, quality of customer support, availability of educational materials, storage solutions, and buyback policies. It's also wise to assess the company's reputation, industry accreditations, and client feedback before deciding.

What are the potential downsides of a gold IRA?

Gold IRAs can enhance portfolio diversity and act as a hedge against inflation, but they come with challenges. These include higher annual fees compared to standard IRAs, costs for storage and insurance, and potential liquidity constraints. Additionally, required minimum distributions start at age 73 for gold IRAs, which may impact planning.

What is the minimum investment for a gold IRA?

Minimum investments vary by company. Noble Gold Investments requires just $2,000, while Augusta Precious Metals sets a $50,000 minimum. Birch Gold Group offers a $10,000 entry point, and Goldco has no minimum requirement, providing flexible options for different budgets.

How do gold IRA companies safeguard my investment?

Trusted gold IRA firms collaborate with secure, insured depositories, such as Delaware Depository or International Depository Services, to store precious metals. These facilities often provide segregated storage and comprehensive insurance through Lloyd's of London. Some gold investment companies also offer offshore storage for added geographic diversification.

About GoldInvestmentPlanner.com

GoldInvestmentPlanner.com serves as a reliable hub for retirement-focused financial guidance and investment evaluations. Launched in 2022, the platform provides detailed assessments of various retirement products, such as Gold IRAs, 401(k) rollovers, cryptocurrency IRAs, and traditional brokerage accounts.

Its research process involves extensive analysis, dedicating hundreds of hours to reviewing factors like transparency, service quality, and overall value. GoldInvestmentPlanner.com maintains editorial independence, ensuring its rankings and reviews remain free from the influence of paid sponsorships.

In addition to its rankings, the site offers comprehensive resources, including guides on protecting assets, hedging against inflation, and planning for long-term retirement goals. This makes GoldInvestmentPlanner.com a valuable tool for investors seeking informed, unbiased retirement strategies.

Contact:

Nathan Schwartzman

nathan@goldinvestmentplanner.com

SOURCE: Gold Investment Planner

data.title: REG - TP ICAP Group plc - Director/PDMR Shareholding
data.description:
RNS Number : 6672K TP ICAP Group plc 29 May 2025  

TP ICAP Group plc

LEI: 2138006YAA7IRVKKGE63

29 May 2025

TP ICAP Group plc (the 'Company' or 'TP ICAP')

Notification of transactions by persons discharging managerial responsibilities ('PDMRs')

On 27 May 2025 the Company received the following notifications relating to the vesting of awards granted on 25 May 2022 over ordinary shares of 25p each of the Company (the "Company Shares") under the rules of the TP ICAP Group plc Restricted Share Plan 2022 (the "2022 RSP").

Vesting of awards

Following the vesting on 27 May 2025 a total of 1,688,467 Company Shares were released by the TP ICAP Employee Benefit Trust ("EBT") to the PDMRs named in the table below, who then elected to sell a number of shares to settle the income tax and national insurance ("NI") liabilities and dealing charges arising upon the vesting of the awards. These market sales of the shares to discharge the income tax, NI liability and dealing charges incurred as a result of the awards vesting were completed on 27 May 2025.

A further two-year post-vesting holding period applies to the awards and the PDMRs listed below will not be permitted to sell any shares during this time.

The table below sets out the number of Company Shares released to each PDMR, the number subsequently sold to discharge the income tax, NI liability and dealing charges incurred as a result of their awards vesting, and the number retained by each PDMR.

data.title: South Africa's SPAR plans to sell Swiss and UK retail businesses
data.description:
South Africa's SPAR Group plans to sell its retail businesses in Switzerland and in the United Kingdom after completing a strategic review of its European operations, the retail and wholesale group said on Thursday.

The group, which owns several SPAR country licences of the Dutch SPAR group, has been trimming its international operations in order to "maximize the return on capital allocated". Last year it sold its loss-making Polish business.

The group said it was in exclusive talks with an established UK-based business over the sale of its UK operation Appleby Westward Group. The potential buyer, which SPAR did not name, was "well positioned to develop and grow AWG in South West England," it said.

In Switzerland, SPAR has been engaging established parties with extensive business interests in the region and experience in European food retail and distribution, it added.

"The group approach has been to engage parties whose interests align with the growth ambitions of the local management teams and retailer partners, and will ensure continuity for employees, suppliers and customers," SPAR said.

The Swiss business, with 300 stores, contributes 16 billion rand ($899 million) to group turnover, while the South West England unit contributes 6 billion rand.

Internationally, SPAR will be left with Ireland, its biggest overseas business, and a joint venture in Sri Lanka.

($1 = 17.7956 rand)

data.title: NFI Maintained at Buy With a $23.00 Price Target at Stifel Canada
data.description:
Stifel Canada on Thursday maintained its buy rating on the shares of NFI Group and its $23.00 price target after the bus manufacturer launched a debt issue.

NFI recently launched a $600 million private offering of second lien senior secured notes under phase 2 of its broader balance sheet restructuring. The key focus for the new second lien notes is to bring down cost of debt by repaying the existing $180 million second lien credit facility as well as the EDC facility, Stifel said.

NFI will also repay a portion of the first lien senior credit facility as part of the transaction, according to Stifel.

"This has been a well-telegraphed and broadly expected second phase of NFI's broader balance sheet restructuring so no major surprises here," analyst Daryl Young wrote. "However, the devil will be in the details around the terms of the notes once the transaction is finalized."

data.title: Max Roof Carmichael Launches Official Website After 15 Years of Service
data.description:
Carmichael, CA, May 29, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Max Roof Carmichael, a trusted name in roofing services in Carmichael, CA, is proud to announce the launch of its official website, www.MaxRoofCarmichael.com. This significant step marks the company's commitment to enhancing customer experience and accessibility in the digital era.

Serving Our Carmichael Neighbors With Their Roofing Needs.

For over 15 years, Max Roof Carmichael has been a cornerstone in the community, providing top-notch roofing solutions for both residential and commercial properties. Known for their exceptional craftsmanship and dedication to customer satisfaction, the company has now expanded its reach by establishing an online presence.

The new website offers a comprehensive overview of the services provided by Max Roof Carmichael, including roof repair, replacement, inspection, and waterproofing solutions. Customers can easily navigate through the site to find detailed information about the materials used, such as asphalt shingles, metal, tile, and TPO, ensuring they make informed decisions about their roofing needs.

"Launching our website is a natural progression for us," said Luis Lara, CEO of Max Roof Carmichael. "We are excited to provide our customers with an easy-to-use platform where they can learn more about our services and get in touch with us effortlessly."

"Our goal has always been to deliver exceptional service and transparency to our clients. With the new website, we are taking a step further in achieving that goal by making information readily available at their fingertips," added Lara.

Max Roof Carmichael's website is designed with user-friendliness in mind, featuring a clean layout and intuitive navigation. Visitors can explore various sections to understand the company's offerings and even request a free roof inspection directly through the site.

This digital transformation not only reflects Max Roof Carmichael's dedication to innovation but also its commitment to maintaining strong relationships with its clients. By embracing technology, the company aims to enhance communication and provide a seamless experience for all its customers.

As Max Roof Carmichael continues to grow and adapt to the changing landscape, the launch of www.MaxRoofCarmichael.com stands as a testament to its enduring commitment to quality and customer satisfaction. The company invites everyone to visit the new website and discover the range of services that have made them a leader in the roofing industry.

About Max Roof Carmichael

We are here tMax Roof Carmichael Serving Carmichael, CA, for over 15 years, Max Roof Carmichael is a trusted local roofing contractor specializing in roof repair, replacement, inspection, and waterproofing solutions. Located at 5709 Marconi Ave Ste N, Carmichael, CA 95608, we proudly offer expert services for residential and commercial properties using top-quality materials like asphalt shingles, metal, tile, and TPO. As a licensed, bonded, and insured company, we are committed to delivering exceptional craftsmanship, transparent pricing, and customer satisfaction. Visit us at MaxRoofCarmichael.com or call (916) 536-7746 for a free roof inspection today!

Press inquiries

Max Roof Carmichael

https://maxroofcarmichael.com

Gene Stygar

gstygar@gmail.com

5709 Marconi Ave Ste N, Carmichael, CA 95608

data.description: e.l.f. Beauty has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $101.50, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Nvidia Secures Mixed Shelf Offering Approval
data.description:
Nvidia filed a mixed shelf registration with the SEC, giving it flexibility to raise capital via equity, debt or other securities over the next three years.

The filing, announced alongside Q1 results that beat estimates and lifted shares 4% post-close, authorizes issuances of common or preferred stock, depositary shares, debt instruments, warrants, stock purchase contracts or units. Nvidia's charter allows for up to 80 billion common shares and 2 million preferred shares; as of April 27, it had 24.39 billion common shares outstanding and zero preferred shares. This shelf vehicle streamlines future funding for everything from R&D to M&A without needing separate SEC registrations.

Investors should care because a mixed shelf offers Nvidia the agility to seize strategic opportunitieslike bolt-on acquisitions or accelerated factory expansionwhile preserving balance-sheet optionality amid an evolving AI landscape.

With this shelf effective immediately, markets will monitor any early follow-on offerings as signals of Nvidia's capital allocation priorities.

data.title: China investigating former head of food and drug watchdog
data.description:
Bi Jingquan, former head of the now-defunct China Food and Drug Administration (CFDA), is being investigated for suspected corruption, China's anti-graft watchdog said on Thursday.

Bi, a member of the standing committee of China's top political advisory body and a deputy director of its committee for economic affairs, is being investigated for suspected serious violations of law and discipline, the watchdog said, without giving details.

Reuters was not able to reach Bi for comment.

Bi, 69, holds a degree in economics from the prestigious Peking University and has served in senior positions in the National Development and Reform Commission, China's state planner, and State Council, China's cabinet.

He was appointed chief of the CFDA in early 2015, a few months before the government launched a major revamp of China's drug review procedure.

During his tenure at CFDA, Bi spearheaded efforts to simplify and expedite the review process for new drugs, and introduced steps to improve the quality of generic drugs.

In 2018, Bi resigned from the CFDA amid fallout from a major vaccine scandal.

data.title: VivoPower International Gets Revised Proposal From Energi to Acquire 51% of Tembo
data.description:
VivoPower International said Thursday that it has received a revised, non-binding offer from Energi Holdings to buy a 51% controlling stake in its electric vehicle subsidiary, Tembo.

The proposed deal values Tembo at $200 million. Energi also supports Tembo's planned merger with Cactus Acquisition (CCTS) and is open to structuring its investment to support that deal. Energi plans to roll its stake into the combined company, while VivoPower would still hold a significant share if the merger goes through, VivoPower said.

Proceeds from any potential sale, like this one, would help pay down debt and could later be returned to shareholders if there are surplus funds, the company added.

The proposal from Energi is indicative and still under review. It depends on conditions like due diligence, legal agreements, and board and shareholder approvals. There's no guarantee the deal will happen, and VivoPower will only provide more updates if necessary, the company said.

Shares of VivoPower rose about 5% in recent trading.

data.title: RSUM’s Wuller: DUA market only in “second innings” of development
data.description:
(The Insurer) - There remains plenty of growth potential in the delegated underwriting authority (DUA) market with the sector only in the “second innings” of the expansion and service it can provide to the specialty insurance industry.

That is the view of Miles Wuller, CEO of Ryan Specialty Underwriting Managers, who told attendees during the Program Manager Conference 2025 that a favourite question from public investors is: “What inning are we in?”

Investors, he said, regularly ask how much carrier interest remains in the space, how much farther can the market grow, and what opportunities still exist in terms of M&A.

And Wuller believes the industry’s development is still in its early stages.

“The more time I spend in this amazing industry, the clearer it becomes to me that we are only in the second innings of the growth and service we can provide to the specialty industry,” the executive said.

When it comes to M&A, Wuller noted that companies operating in the DUA space continue to scale, with RSUM’s parent Ryan Specialty itself having been very active in its bid to build out its operations over the past 18 months.

The company undertook seven deals last year, including those of US Assure, Castel Underwriting Agencies and certain P&C-focused MGUs owned by Ethos Specialty.

This year has also seen Ryan Specialty acquire alternative risk-focused MGU USQRisk, and Velocity Risk Underwriters.

During his presentation, Wuller claimed the industry is “far too loose with our terminology”, while the RSUM chief also noted that DUA companies’ lifespan, which historically has been closely aligned with founders’ involvement, has been extended because of their greater scale.

“Programs and MGUs and delegated underwriting authority are often used interchangeably. The reality is, they're quite distinct, and a broad-brush descriptor hasn't been helpful in making our case known in the industry and better understood,” he said.

As Wuller noted, programs, which he described as “often retail-distributed, typically admitted package policies with lower limits”, have earned a long-standing appreciation by the carrier community.

When the industry looks upon the recent rapid growth of the delegated underwriting authority sector, Wuller said “the renaissance … of the last 10 to 15 years, which has certainly been assisted by the golden age of E&S, has unequivocally been the eruption of the true MGA and MGU entity”.

MGUs, he said, have “stepped in” to innovate in the some of the most complex, high-hazard and limit-requiring industry segments.

“MGUs, with their dramatic investment, people and process, have expanded into the white space of the industry.”

While the DUA market has recorded unprecedented growth in recent years, Wuller said “it’s critical to lengthen the lifespan of the average MGU”.

“Historically, very few MGUs have made it past the original founder (because they have) just too much concentrated knowhow (and) too many concentrated relationships.

“Scaled platforms have brought the ability to help founders not only reach new levels of achievement, but ultimately perpetuate the business into the future.”

data.title: SentinelOne Likely to Face Challenges Despite 'Attractive' Endpoint Market, BofA Securities Says
data.description:
SentinelOne is likely to face challenges ahead, although the endpoint market continues to be an "attractive domain," BofA Securities said in a Thursday note.

The company has been reporting "uninspiring" results for a few quarters now and has lowered its fiscal 2026 outlook for the second consecutive quarter, analyst Tal Liani wrote.

SentinelOne attributed the fiscal 2026 guidance cut to "macro uncertainty and deal fluctuations," the analyst said.

Additionally, Liani said that the business scenarios look "weaker" as net new annual recurring revenue fell 26% in fiscal Q1 from a year earlier.

BofA Securities downgraded SentinelOne to neutral from buy due to "weak trends and lackluster guidance" and lowered its price target to $21 from $24.

Shares of the company were down 10% in recent Thursday trading.

data.title: Bacardi’s Talent Management VP on Blending a 160-Year Family Heritage With a Modern Twist
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / May 29, 2025 / By Tony Case

Originally published on WorkLife

As global VP of talent management for the past decade-plus at one of the world's best-known spirits brands, John McCusker has witnessed firsthand the challenges faced by HR teams everywhere - but with a twist.

At a time when conversations about the workplace tend to revolve around AI, RTO, the impact of President Trump's rapid-fire directives and other changes in the way business is done, McCusker remains focused on something fundamentally different at Bacardi Ltd.: preserving human connection.

"How do we keep humanity in the workplace?" he asks, recalling conversations with fellow HR leaders at a recent conference. While everyone else there was fixated on the implementation of AI, McCusker found himself wondering about a more pressing matter: how companies can deploy AI to help line leaders connect with their people on a more personal level.

That thinking comes at a time of intense challenges for the spirits trade - particularly as Trump's trade policies threaten to turn that industry, as so many others, upside down.

The emphasis on people is not some touchy-feely proposition. A people-first approach to talent management is at the heart of the 160-year-old, family-owned spirits marketer, beginning with how the company views its employees - affectionately called primos, Spanish for cousins. The term originated years ago when the chairman was addressing senior leadership and noted, "When we talk to one another as family members, we say ‘Hola, primo!' - because when we think about you as leadership and the rest of the organization, we see you as extended family."

"In families there are no annual performance reviews - you have just-in-time, dynamic, in-the-moment, authentic conversations about what's working and what isn't working."

- John McCusker, VP of talent management, Bacardi Ltd.

At Bacardi, that isn't just corporate speak - it is at the core of how talent management functions. As McCusker explains, "Do you sit down with your spouse or your partner at the end of the year and have a formal year-end review? In families there are no annual performance reviews - you have just-in-time, dynamic, in-the-moment, authentic conversations about what's working and what isn't working."

It is that perspective that catalyzed the creation of "Let's Talk," Bacardi's distinctive approach to performance management, launched in 2016. Rather than traditional performance reviews, the program encourages natural, employee-driven conversations that happen across the year. "We said the world's changing at a pace that we probably need to rethink," McCusker said. "Is it really relevant to say on the first of April, ‘Here's what we're going to do' - and then the world changes and you look back on the 31st of March to something that very quickly became out of date?"

The company's timing executing an ongoing, fluid dialogue with its people couldn't have been more perfect. When the pandemic hit and Bacardi's workforce went remote, it meant the company already had a performance system in place built on trust, authentic communication and regular conversations. The results speak for themselves: employee engagement scores improved after the implementation of the policy, while metrics related to performance management showed marked improvement. Team members reported high satisfaction with both the frequency and quality of conversations about expectations and career development, according to McCusker.

Bacardi's talent approach is anchored in what McCusker calls the "Three F's"-Fearless, Family and Founders. The "Fearless" component carries dual significance, meaning the courage to, as McCusker puts it, "run through walls to deliver objectives," as well as the vulnerability to "put your hand up and say, ‘I need help.'"

The emphasis on authentic leadership runs like a thread through all the company's development programs, McCusker explains. "How do we move leaders from being heroes to humans?" he said, noting that corporate leaders are encouraged to embrace their own humanity and vulnerability versus maintaining some façade of perfection.

"John brings a level of expertise, empathy and vulnerability that creates an open dialogue central to creating a positive workforce where talent can flourish."

- Tony Latham, regional president, Bacardi North America

Meanwhile, the "Family" element manifests in an "all-in mentality," encouraging employees to think "Bacardi first, then team, then you." The approach aligns incentives with collective success rather than short-term individual gains - a marked departure from the shareholder-first mentality of many publicly traded organizations.

For a company with a century and a half under its belt, balancing tradition with innovation can be a tall order. McCusker sees it not as a contradiction, however, but as complementary. "It's not an either-or - it's and," he said, with team members encouraged to ask: "How do you celebrate the past as you build an even richer and more successful future?"

Read more

View additional multimedia and more ESG storytelling from Bacardi-Martini, Inc. on 3blmedia.com.

data.title: European Equities Traded in the US as American Depositary Receipts Modestly Higher in Thursday Trading
data.description:
European equities traded in the US as American depositary receipts were heading moderately higher late Thursday morning, rising 0.32% to 1,451.76 on the S&P Europe Select ADR Index.

From continental Europe, the gainers were led by biopharmaceutical companies Cellectis and Grifols , which rose 2.5% and 2.1% respectively. They were followed by pharmaceutical company Ascendis Pharma , which was up 0.5%.

The decliners from continental Europe were led by biopharmaceutical company DBV Technologies and biotech firm Evaxion , which dropped 8.9% and 6.5% respectively. They were followed by semiconductor company Sequans Communications , which dropped 1.6%, accommodations booking company trivago , which fell 1.5%, and petroleum refiner Equinor , down 0.9%.

From the UK and Ireland, the gainers were led by biotech firms Autolus Therapeutics and Trinity Biotech , which advanced 7.9% and 5.5% respectively. They were followed by biopharmaceutical companies Bicycle Therapeutics and Adaptimmune Therapeutics , which increased 4.4% and 1.3% respectively.

The decliners from the UK and Ireland were led by biopharmaceutical companies NuCana and Biodexa Pharmaceuticals , which lost 4% and 1.9% respectively. They were followed by education publisher Pearson and tobacco company British American Tobacco , which were both off 0.8%.

data.title: Darling Ingredients to Dual List on New York, NYSE Texas Exchanges
data.description:
Darling Ingredients will soon begin to trade on both the New York Stock Exchange and the NYSE Texas electronic stock market, the recycler of food waste and other agricultural products said Thursday.

The company did not say when the dual listing was expected to take effect. The stock will continue to trade using its DAR ticker on both exchanges, it said.

NYSE Texas opened for business on March 31 after Intercontinental Exchange elected to move its NYSE Chicago equities market to Dallas and rebranded the exchange.

Shares of Darling Ingredients and Intercontinental Exchange were down 1.4% and 0.1%, respectively, in recent trading.

data.title: FTSE MIB Struggles to Hold 40,000
data.description:
The FTSE MIB erased early gains to hover below the 40,000 threshold on Thursday, tracking other European benchmarks as markets reconsidered the outlook on European trade.

While European equities initially rose after a US Commercial Court blocked most global tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump, the Milan exchange gave up gains as White House pledges to pursue other tariff means erased optimism of no trade barriers.

Media and utilities stocks lead the declines across Europe.

In Italy, Leonardo, Generali, Prysmian, and Buzzi are among the worst performers, with Buzzi.

On the other hand, Unipol, Brunello Cucinelli, Inwit, and Mediobanca lead gains on the index.

About RM Infrastructure Income PLC

RM Infrastructure Income Plc is a closed-ended investment trust established to invest in a portfolio of bespoke, higher-yielding secured debt instruments.

The Company aims to generate attractive and regular dividends and positive social impact through loans sourced or originated by the Investment Manager with a degree of inflation protection through index-linked returns where appropriate. Loans in which the Company invests are predominantly secured against assets such as real estate or plant and machinery and/or income streams such as account receivables.

For more information, please see https://rm-funds.co.uk/rm-infrastructure-income/

About RM Funds

RM Funds is an alternative asset manager. Founded in 2010, with offices in Edinburgh, and London, the firm manages capital on behalf of institutional investors, multi-asset allocators, wealth managers and retail investors. RM Funds is a delivery partner to the British Business Bank in connection with the Coronavirus Business Interruption Loan Scheme. RM Funds is a trading name of RM Capital Markets Limited.

RM Funds is a signatory to the Principles of Responsible Investment.

data.title: Salesforce Stock Slides on Acquisition News. Strong Earnings Weren't Enough. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Levine

Salesforce reported better-than-expected first-quarter earnings on Wednesday afternoon. Its shares were rising in after-hours trading.

Adjusted earnings-per-share were $2.58 versus Wall Street's consensus estimate of $2.55, according to FactSet, and up from $2.44 last year. Revenue for the quarter reached $9.83 billion, above expectations of $9.75 billion, and up 7.6% on the year.

Salesforce also gave strong guidance for the second quarter, above Wall Street expectations, and raised its fiscal 2026 outlook.

On Wednesday, Salesforce announced its intent to buy Informatica, a company that provides data management and integration services, which will support Salesforce's AI play, Agentforce. Agents are software that uses language models to automate a series of tasks from a simple prompt, but those agents need company data that is clean and ready.

"Bringing Informatica in helps complete the data picture for a lot of our enterprise customers, stitching the broader set of data together," Salesforce Executive Vice President of Investor Relations Mike Spencer told Barron's. "It helps stitch non-Salesforce data together in a much tighter fashion so that the agent answering questions of the employee or the customer is more accurate and complete because the data set underneath is more tightly bound together."

Salesforce stock was up 5% in late trading following the release.

This is breaking news. Read a preview of Salesforce's earnings below and check back for more analysis soon.

When Salesforce reports its first-quarter earnings on Wednesday afternoon, it will be another marker in the dual transitions the company is undergoing. Wall Street analysts expect adjusted earnings-per-share of $2.55, up 4% from last year, on revenue of $9.75 billion, up 7%.

From fiscal 2006 to 2022, Salesforce's annual revenue growth exceeded 24%. Sales expansion has slowed, a natural path for a mature software company. But it also means growing cash flow and cash return to shareholders. The last three years of buybacks have reduced the diluted share count by 2.7%, and the company initiated a dividend last year.

But Microsoft was able to keep growing by adding other products and services, most notably its Azure cloud unit. Oracle is following the same playbook. Salesforce's play to renew growth is to augment existing software with AI services, Agentforce. Agents are software that uses AI language models to automate a series of complex tasks from a simple prompt, bringing a new level of automation to Salesforce's customer relations management software.

Sales may not be going well here — Salesforce has broken with its subscription-based revenue model, and is allowing customers to pay as they go for Agentforce.

To support Agentforce, on Tuesday Salesforce announced that it intends to acquire Informatica for $8 billion, pending regulatory approval. The Salesforce agents require customer data to do their jobs, and Informatica software cleans and integrates the data, creating a "single source of truth" for the agents.

There will likely be pointed questions during the earnings call about this merger. Informatica grows in the mid-single digits, so it won't help Salesforce's growth profile. Informatica software has no integration with Salesforce like its competitor SnapLogic does, so that will have to be built from scratch.

Analyst Howard Ma of Guggenheim Partners points to another risk. "Salesforce has a long history of taking many years to integrate acquired companies (e.g., ExactTarget, DemandWare, MuleSoft, Tableau, Slack), some of which have never been fully integrated from a technology perspective," he wrote in a Tuesday note to clients.

Salesforce says the merger will add to profits beginning in the second year after the deal closes.

Write to Adam Levine at adam.levine@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Best Buy CEO: Company Has Already Passed Through Tariff-Related Price Increases — WSJ
data.description:
By Sarah Nassauer

At electronics retailer Best Buy, Wednesday's surprise trade court ruling doesn't change much for now, said Chief Executive Corie Barry.

The retailer has already increased prices on some products to offset tariff-related cost increases. Best Buy also has worked to offset the impact by negotiating with its suppliers to reduce costs, adjusting the products it sells and expanding the number of countries from which it imports goods, said Barry.

"I don't think there is anything we would do differently based on the news overnight," she said on a call with reporters Thursday. "What I've really tried to work with the team on is to not actually overreact to any given moment."

In the few weeks since Best Buy's price increases, demand for those items hasn't fallen, she said.

  • Read more on Best Buy's earnings and outlook, which has hit the stock today.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).
